name: Xylitol
description: Xylitol is a naturally occurring five-carbon sugar alcohol found in most plant material, including many fruits and vegetables. It is widely used as a sugar substitute and in "sugar-free" food products. Xylitol is used prevent middle ear infections (otitis media) in young children, as an energy source, and as a sugar substitute for people with diabetes, due to its negligible effects on blood sugar and insulin. It also exhibits some oral health benefits. Since it is a nonfermentable sugar alcohol, xylitol is added to some chewing gums and other oral care products to prevent tooth decay and dry mouth.
